text.title: QBR Deck Template Services Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding QBR Deck Template Services plus the universal skip/run routing pair (referenced by the decision tree).

rule.statement: Each QBR deck MUST follow the fixed 10-slide shape (cover, outcomes, metrics, wins, risks, asks, finance, retention plan, next quarter, owners); custom slide orders MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Custom shapes force the client to re-orient each quarter and signal weak operating discipline; fixed shape is the contract.

rule.statement: Every slide MUST carry a named owner accountable for its content; orphan slides MUST be deleted.
rule.rationale: Orphan slides drift to generic platitudes; named-owner is the structural gate against deck rot.

rule.statement: Every metric or claim MUST anchor to evidence (dashboard URL, ticket ID, transcript timestamp); unanchored claims MUST be removed.
rule.rationale: Unanchored claims invite client dispute; evidence anchors make the deck a defensible record.

rule.statement: The deck MUST review outcomes against the prior-quarter plan (delta, root cause, next move); pure output recaps (hours billed) MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Output-only QBRs reinforce time-billing perception and erode strategic positioning; outcome review is the value frame.

rule.statement: The deck MUST have a dedicated Asks slide listing 1-3 explicit asks (intros, references, scope changes, budget); decks without Asks MUST be returned for revision.
rule.rationale: QBRs without asks underuse client goodwill; the dedicated slide is the structural prompt against polite over-modesty.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` do not ALL hold OR any disqualifier in `## Skip If` fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and document the skip reason in the cycle artefact.
rule.rationale: A minimum-form decision tree must route 'preconditions-not-satisfied' to skip; this rule is the canonical target.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `## Applies If` ALL hold AND no disqualifier fires, the agent MUST run the methodology end-to-end and produce the artefact specified in 02-output-contract.xml.
rule.rationale: Pair to skip-this-methodology; together they form the always-present minimal route.
